### 6. How memory safety CVEs differ between Rust and C/C++

- Source: [kobzol.github.io](https://kobzol.github.io/rust/2026/06/15/how-memory-safety-cves-differ-between-rust-and-c-cpp.html)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-18T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Rust eliminates traditional memory corruption CVEs but produces different types of memory safety bugs versus C/C++; the languages have different CVE profiles, not just different counts.

Builder/operator takeaway: Useful calibration for evaluating Rust security claims in tech stack decisions.

Uncertainty: Methodology details and dataset size not available from content snippet.
